reviews.text:
Rob is a pleasant person to have as an attorney.
Took care of my back in 2017 and now currently helping me out with another case.
Always making sure I'm okay and not feeling overwhelmed. Rob is so easy to speak with and I see him as my friend always having my back.
Thanks Rob for all you've done and continue to do .

Our family hired Rob Shindler to handle a case and we could not be more pleased with the results. Rob's extensive knowledge of the law, years of experience and compassion towards our family was second to none. Rob was clear on his approach and the steps he would take to achieve the best outcome in our first meeting. Rob was always available to answer any questions or concerns we had during the process. If we texted a question, he would not text back but instead take the time to call so he could communicate with us on a personal level. Rob worked extremely hard to achieve the best outcome. I highly recommend Rob Shindler.

I found myself facing what seemed like an impossible legal situation, one that left me feeling completely overwhelmed. From our first meeting, Rob changed everything. He took a case that I thought was difficult and navigated it with remarkable skill and precision.
What truly sets him apart isn't just his legal expertise, it's his personality. He has this rare ability to explain complex legal matters in a way that actually makes sense, and his calm, trustworthy demeanor made me feel like I could put my life in his hands (also has a great sense of humor). Throughout the entire process, he transformed what could have been an overwhelming ordeal into a manageable journey.
Thanks to his expertise and dedication, we achieved an outcome I never thought possible. If you're looking for a lawyer who combines exceptional legal knowledge with genuine human understanding, I couldn't recommend him more!

I wanted to take a moment to share with you my recent experiences with a remarkable individual, Mr. Rob Shindler. Throughout the challenging times brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, I had the privilege of getting to know Mr. Shindler on both a professional and personal level.

I first encountered Mr. Shindler in his professional capacity as a lawyer, and it quickly became evident that he was not just any attorney but an exceptional one. His dedication to his clients and unwavering commitment to excellence set him apart from the rest.

Over time, what began as a professional relationship transcended into a close friendship. Mr. Shindler's genuine care and concern for those around him are truly commendable. He consistently goes above and beyond to provide assistance and support, demonstrating a level of dedication that is rare to find.

One aspect of Mr. Shindler's character that has left a lasting impression on me is his mantra of "paso por paso" - taking things step by step. This philosophy not only reflects his meticulous approach to his work but also serves as a reminder of his patience and thoroughness in everything he does.

Moreover, Mr. Shindler's accessibility and willingness to help are truly remarkable. He is always just a phone call away, ready to assist and offer guidance, even outside of regular office hours. His reliability and genuine interest in helping others are qualities that I deeply admire.

In conclusion, I can confidently attest to Mr. Rob Shindler's outstanding professional skills, unwavering dedication, and exceptional character. He is not just an excellent lawyer but a trustworthy friend who I am grateful to have in my life.

I hired Keith Shindler after a terrible car accident and cannot be more grateful. He made me feel like my case was a top priority and made sure to keep me informed through the entire process. He answered every question I had and reassured me that he would get the maximum amount for my situation which is exactly what he did. i cannot recommend Shindler and Shindler enough!

I have known Robert for a little while personally. He developed a business and friendship relationship with my late father. There has not been a time where i try to reach out to Robert and he doesn't answer, and when he does not answer he ALWAYS reaches out when hes free. The most beautiful thing my father left me when he left this world were a handful of reliable people i can count on when i need help and whole heartedly Robert is one of those people. He goes above and beyond. If he cant help me personally he send me in the right direction with no self gain no alternate motive. This guy is a beautiful human being. And im glad j can call him my friend.

When my husband and I found ourselves needing legal representation, we were fortunate enough to find a lawyer who went above and beyond our expectations. From our very first consultation, it was clear that we weren't just another case number, we were people who needed genuine support and guidance through a challenging time.What truly set our lawyer apart was his ability to build trust while maintaining absolute professionalism. He took the time to explain complex legal procedures in terms we could understand, never rushing through explanations or dismissing our concerns. Each step of the process was carefully outlined, helping us feel confident in our decisions and the direction of our case.The level of care we received was remarkable. Rob was consistently responsive, keeping us updated on developments and ensuring we were well informed about our options. He demonstrated a genuine commitment to protecting our interests, often anticipating potential issues before they arose and developing solutions that fit our needs. Rob definitely put our mind at ease throughout our process. We honestly couldn't recommend  a better lawyer. Rob, Once again we are truly thankful !

Mr Shindler is a great guy! I've been knowing Robert for over a decade and he's always been helpful! Responds fast and professional. Even if your issue is out of his field, he will still help you out the best way he can. I really appreciate everything he has helped out with! 👍 ⚖️ 💯

First and foremost I wanna thank God for this great, wonderful being. Someone who doesn't just give me legal advice, but has advised me through life. He didn't stop fighting for me. I never trusted anyone as much as I trust this man with my freedom. I was looking at 21 to 45 years and he got the case dismissed. Thanks to Rob I was able to change my life around. Thank you God and Thank you Rob. Someone who I not only consider a friend but someone who I also look up to as a role model. 🙏

I had an excellent experience with Shindler & Shindler, specifically with Rob. From the start, he was professional, knowledgeable, and genuinely caring about our situation. He not only took the time to explain everything clearly but also showed real compassion towards my wife and son, making sure we all felt supported and informed. It’s rare to find a lawyer who is both highly skilled and truly compassionate, but Rob exceeded our expectations.I highly recommend Shindler & Shindler to anyone in need of legal guidance.

My deepest thanks to Rob and Keith Schindler Law Firm for their phenomenal support in resolving my car accident case. After the accident, I didn't think I needed legal counsel. Im so glad my husband found their page and told me to give their office a call. The first consultation was Keith Schindler happened almost instantly via video call. He listened to me and made sure that he understood my side of the story. He also provided great advice and kept following up with me to make sure I was recovering well. Rob Schindler was also pulled into my case, and together, they fought the insurance company very hard on my behalf.
Rob kept me posted along the way, leaving no stone unturned with every detail. They worked diligently, ensuring every piece of my case was reviewed, leading to a great result in the end. I'm very appreciative of their support and constant communication along the way. They are a great team. I would highly recommend their services to anyone needing legal support after any type of car accident. You won't regret it!
Best attorney in town period.
Wonderful people to work with !

Kevin was referred to me by another attorney to work on an appeals case in tandem. To be honest, I have yet to meet him! But, I can tell you he did a wonderful job for me. Through only phone calls with me, Attorney Sommer grasped the case and situation and took charge. I felt in very competent hands. He was up front about the procedure and kept me informed. I would not hesitate to hire again. I hope to meet him someday too! And my appeals case was perfect!
Very nice attorney and so is his wife. He makes you feel very comfortable when speaking to him. Mr. Sommer's is very thorough.

Kevin Sommer has been our lawyer for 10 years and is wonderful. I would highly recommend him to anyone. He is thorough, attentive and goes above and beyond explaining the different avenues of our needs as they relate to law.He is, by far, the nicest lawyer you will ever meet.

Mr Sommer's "investigation" process seemed very lackadaisical. 1 meeting with each family in his office. Never did home visits after they were requested, and the only outside source he talked with was a biased party, which he called the day before court. He was late to literally EVERY meeting and court date. Kevin seemed like a nice person, but he did not thoroughly look into our case at all.

Attorney Sommer is a true professional. He's kind, thorough & shows genuine concern for his client. He is conscientious, frank, an attentive listener & most importantly, he is fair. I absolutely recommend Attorney Sommer.
Called for consultation on a case.  Mr. Sommer was very friendly and helpful.
I had a catastrophic accident at work;  Tom got me through it and got me the best benefits possible. This was a terrible period of my life and Tom got me through it. I would highly recommend him.

This man is awesome!!!! He took my case and made everything super easy for me. He is not local to me but still managed to handle everything without problems.  I will recommend Tom Mazur every chance I get.

I recently had the privilege of working with Tom Mazur at his law office, and I must say, my experience was nothing short of exceptional. Tom's expertise and professionalism not only helped me navigate a challenging legal situation but also made the entire process remarkably comfortable.

Mr. Mazur is an exceptional lawyer and an even better man.  He has helped me with a workers comp case, police injury/disability case, and a PSEBA case. Tom gives a client personal service and listens to your problems/issues at hand, unlike huge law firms that don’t care about their clients and barely have time for them. You will not go wrong if you choose The Law Offices of Thomas Mazur.

Attorney Mazur handled both my workman's compensation and my duty disability pension hearing. Attorney Mazur was professional and always took my calls or called right back. Attorney  Mazur  settled my workman's compensation case quickly and got me a good settlement.  Attorney Mazur then prepared me for my disability hearing, which I won. Attorney Mazur is organized,  friendly and professional.  I would highly recommend him to anyone that needs an attorney.  Thank you Attorney Mazur.
Tom explained every step and gave me assurance that he will take care of my case and he was always communicating me when something new came up about my case , thank you Tom !
Next level legal stuff going on here

Mr. Mazur is an outstanding attorney who helped through a process no firefighter wants to go through - a disability hearing. You are already suffering from the physical pain of injuries or other medical issues that have a lasting effect on your mental health. Tom was there for me and my family every step of the way. He understands firefighters because he is one also. I would recommend Thomas Mazur to anyone, not just firefighters.

The Law Offices of Thomas Mazur is an incredible resource and zealous advocate in personal injury, workers compensation, police and fire disability pensions, public safety discipline, real estate and estate planning matters. As a fellow attorney I frequently consult with Mr. Mazur on complicated matters and refer him to all of my contacts in need of top-tier representation in matters I cannot specifically address. From my contacts, I have heard nothing but extremely positive comments about Mr. Mazur’s responsiveness, personality, advocacy and success.

Tom is the best attorney I have ever dealt with.  He communicates well and did everything he could to help me in the best way.  He is always looking out for his clients.  I would recommend him to anyone looking for legal help.
Everyone there cares about you and makes you feel at ease. They only care about taking care of their clients. They are the only law firm I would ever trust

I had difficulty finding an attorney in the Rockford area that understood how to stop online attacks from lead generation companies. Phil sent numerous cease and desist letters that were highly effective. These lead generation companies were using Dynamic Keyword Insertion tools from the search engines on pay per click marketing campaigns. What this meant was when people searched for my company's keywords the tools put that exact keyword into the ads. Of course, the search engines shift the liability exposure to the end user so this made it difficult to get any assistance from the search engines. We even tried submitting our registered mark to the search engines so they could exclude our keyword. I am so thankful for what Phil has done to help curb this issue for us. At one point 3 of the 4 displayed ads said they were our company using our name in the ads when in fact they were NOT. We have had to sue companies in federal court because they reneged on our settlements. But, because of Phil's initial correspondences and ongoing monitoring efforts we were able to recover multiple 6 figures from these companies stealing our leads. In an effort to mitigate losses we pulled back on our marketing until we could get this lead misdirection practice to stop. At one point we estimate 60-75% of our organic traffic was misdirected. Today all of the illegal pay per click ads have stopped. This was a long process and Phil was there for every step. Thank you Phil!
reviews.ownerResponse: Dan, assisting you and IKA has been one of the true highlights of my career. Thank you for your kind words and I continue to look forward to working with you and your team.

I’ve been working with Black & Jones in Rockford, IL for 8+ months with no success obtaining treatment—still no MRI despite a physician referral. Initially told it was due to a “bad video angle,” then Tracy Jones claimed the footage was clear, contradicting her associates claim, and also my account. They’ve ignored my emails and my ongoing injuries. I strongly advise anyone needing a Workmen’s Comp attorney to avoid them—they won’t fight for your treatment or compensation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your concerns. We are truly sorry to hear that you feel your questions and injuries have been ignored—that is never the experience we want for any client.
After reviewing your file, we do want to clarify one important point: every email and communication we received from you was responded to promptly, including days when multiple messages were sent. We strive to ensure that our clients always receive timely updates, even when circumstances limit the outcome they hope for.
Regarding medical treatment, we understand how difficult it is to wait for care when you are in pain. Unfortunately, in workers’ compensation cases, treatment decisions and authorizations are controlled by the law and the insurance carrier—not by our office. While we advocate as strongly as possible, there are times when the legal process restricts what can be obtained and how quickly it can happen. We know that can be incredibly frustrating, and we regret that we were not able to meet your expectations within those legal constraints.
We remain committed to advocating for every client within the scope of the law, and we value the opportunity to discuss your concerns directly. If you would like to talk, we welcome you to contact our office.

reviews.text: I had to call to get status for my case all the time. One and a half years of frustration. I believe they didn’t work for me. The settlement was not what I should have received. They didn’t fight for me at all. Tracy was rude and condescending towards me.  I’m so upset, depressed and will never be healthy again. I do not recommend them at all. I trusted them and they obviously didn’t care. I never met with them, talked on phone a couple times. They got their 20% for fee and it’s regrettable that I will never be healthy again.
reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Harris, I am sorry to see your review. I am sure that it has been difficult dealing with the injury and your condition. We strive to do the best we can for each client, including you. Throughout our time representing you, we had multiple communications and you were always welcome to come in for appointments rather than communicate via email or phone. I am sorry you regret not taking us up on that offer. I will keep this in mind and recommend it more strongly to clients in the future to avoid similar regrets. I am sure you are still dealing with your underlying situation and that has made it difficult for you. But please recall that we did present you with several options as to how you could move forward with your case. Ultimately we had to follow your instructions when you elected to settle the case. I hope that one day you do not regret your own decision. I truly wish you the best in the future.

reviews.text:
Black and Jones law firm gave me a wonderful experience between emails, paperwork, and working with their attorneys and office staff as well.  Stephanie Siebold, who helped with my court date, was extremely helpful. The court date, in my opinion, went very well and was actually the easiest part of the entire application for disability process. Stephanie was so knowledgeable, very familiar with my case, and asked all of the important questions during the hearing, so that way the vocational specialist and the judge understood my situation and disabilities. The judge and vocational specialist/doctor didn't have to question me a bunch on their own.

I had tried to find an attorney and law office to help me on my own, but did not have good luck finding an office who would take my case. Atticus was also a huge help and blessing to me to help connect me with Black and Jones. Although they are located in Illinois and I am in Wisconsin and I did not get the opportunity to meet them, the experience was still phenomenal and I would highly recommend both Black and Jones Attorneys at Law and Atticus online who matched me with the right law firm for me, who specializes in cases like mine.

I opted for the hearing to do it on the phone only, over in person or zoom. I felt the most comfortable with them being unable to see me. The judge who lead my hearing was located in Michigan as well. He was very polite and friendly and this was expected.

Thanks again.

With every court date, Laura knew exactly what to do next and was very straightforward on what to expect. She has a very bright and inviting personality and a even bigger heart for her clients situations.

I learned of updated court dates by going to the county website. Rarely return calls or emails unless being critical of how things are beinf handled. I actually had more cases updates through opposing client than I did this office. All the while getting billed $350 a shot to review the case file when nothing had changed in almost a year and multiple court dates. If Laura responds to this review it will be the first time I've spoken to her in a year. Any other business that runs like this one would be out of business in a year. Laura represented my ex-husband as counsel in our divorce proceedings. He was so very lucky to have her representation. I watched Laura in court and saw her competence come through with poise and a strong understanding of the law. She represented my ex well and seemed to do her best to avoid litigation, rather than encourage it. This is a huge consideration when you are looking for a family law attorney and a characteristic you must not overlook.

Furthermore, her professionalism spoke loud, and her representation was steadfast. There are very few attorneys I would list as top in their profession, but Laura is definitely one of them (her opposing counsel on my behalf being in that group, as well. Court was very (emphasis) interesting!)

Yes, I breathed a sigh of relief when my ex removed her as counsel just a few months into our 3.5 year ordeal of a divorce. Unfortunately, his follow-up choices paled in comparison to the representation I saw he received from Laura.

While there are many choices you  may be considering when facing the tumultuous onset of a life-changing event such as divorce or other family legal matters, you should make sure to include Laura in those you interview during your search for the perfect fit to represent you in court. I have no qualms in stating that I rank her as one of the top family law attorneys in Rockford - as do many others. Moreover, I admire her courtroom presence, her work ethic, the support she gives to her community, and her straight-up, over-the-charts smarts. She is one of  a handful who have inadvertently influenced me to pursue a career in law simply by the work they've done in and out of the courtroom.

Now, how many lawyers have the opposing party leave a positive review? I'm not sure one can be complimented much further,. Laura earned it.

reviews.ownerResponse:
This Office prides itself on its reputation in the community. When I walk into almost any courtroom in the areas I service, if I have been there before, that Judge and that Prosecutor know that I am upfront and tough. If I say the sky is blue, they don't need to run to a window and check. My word is good.
As attorneys, we do not make the ultimate decisions on most things. We are like the GPS that gives a client what we feel is the best direction in which to travel, but ultimately it is the client's decision in which to travel. We, as attorneys, also do not sentence anyone to jail. That is in the discretion of the Judge.
Unfortunately, first time felons are not excluded from going to jail. It is one of the factors that a Judge will consider, but if the crime that the person either admits to committing or is found guilty of committing is of a nature in which its severity demands jail, even a first-time felon can go to jail.
I do not possess a magic wand. Some of my clients have gone to jail. Some of them went to jail by a negotiated plea in which they are in agreement and some are sentenced by a Judge after a trial. I have represented the innocent and the guilty. I have represented a speeding ticket all the way up to someone charged with Murder.
I strive to make all my clients happy. I fight to get each and every client the very best result in their case.

I am a novice on how our criminal justice system actually works even though I am 72 tears of age.  A person is considered guilty if arrested in this country and has to   prove their innocent.    The courts attitude toward people that have been arrested is you would not be here if you were not guilty.  In order to prove your innocent or you have a case that is morally correct you need a good attorney. trouble Brendan Caver helped guide me through all the court appearances and worked with me on my request to use an outside investigator.  Brendan was ready to fight in the court room for my improper arrest.  I believe the court realized that Brendan Caver was going to go to the mat for me and “dismissed the case.”   I understand what the people are saying when they state the police should be defunded because of their tendency to disbelieve people.    Brendan Caver reputation precedes him I believe in his staunch and fair representation of his clients.  It is reviews like this one that helped convince me to enlist the services of Brendan Caver for my defense.  I hope those of you that read this review are of the same thought.

reviews.text:
I submitted roughly three inquires through the website in the past month or so, but never received a response of any kind. I called the office yesterday to follow up, and was told they could not represent me due to a conflict of interest. I asked for explaination of the conflict of interest or recommendations for another attorny. I was informed they were unable to cite the conflict of interest, then the call was ended. No explaination or recommations given.

I understand the importance of maintaining ethical standards, and in my own professional role, it is possible for relationships to  potentially overlap and create such a concern. If I had cause to believe the claim was valid, then its an easy move in the next direction. What I don't understand is the unwillingness to be transparent or helpful. It felt like I was a burden on who I was speaking with or that I was expected to know things I didn't. For a parent seeking help for a distressful situation, the dismissiveness and lack of empathy was palpatable and disappointing.

At best this looks like a misunderstanding, at worst it feels like discrimination. The customer service experience of being declined for an unsubstantiated reason with no opportunity for clarification was frustrating. It felt like I was wrong for wanting to advocate for myself and my child, and rather than offering hope of getting experienced representation,  my experiences and beliefs that family court looks down on fathers were further reinforced instead.

Regarding the response:

This is a prime example of DARVO (deny, attack, reverse victim/offender), the same tactics used by the Trump and Co., and the response follows this to a T.

The claim of multiple contacts over many years is unsubtantiated and simply false. One call approaximately three years ago is not multiple calls.

It is fact that the firm does not currently employ anyone with whom a conflict of interst could occur. No need to assume.

The accusation of me speaking aggressively is also 100% false. I speak how I write.

One previous review that I chose to remove is not "multiple." It was seeing the false accusations in their first response that prompted a more detailed response.

It's baffling that a firm would prefer to perpetuate a negative interaction with defensiveness and lies rather than a form of resolution that could be easily achieved. The inability to respond to criticism professionally is a signifcant tell.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Nathan. You have contacted us multiple times over many years, and we have told you every time that we cannot represent you. You have made your own assumptions about why we can't represent you and have spoken to our staff members aggressively and you have left us multiple negative reviews because we have followed the ethical practices of our profession. You have refused to take no for an answer despite there being dozens of other firms that you could consult with instead of us.  We represent fathers successfully regularly, as our reputation and our five-star reviews reflect. We do not represent people who treat our staff poorly.

reviews.text:
Was in a position over the weekend to assist my Partner with speaking to an attorney about her “in flight” divorce.  Received a referral from an attorney friend for a lawyer at this firm (Zachary Townsend).  Reviews were just OK, but I trusted the referral.

Reached out via email over the weekend asking to setup a consultation (my partner was working over the weekend).  On the Pro side, someone from the office left me a voicemail on Sunday touching base and asking me to call back on Monday AM.   On Monday AM I called, thanked the person for reaching out on a Sunday.  She asked what the consultation was about, and I shared that it was a divorce that is about a year into the process.  We made an appointment for several days in the future.   I was asked to fill out an “intake sheet” and paid for the consultation in advance.

This is where things got bizarre.

I completed the intake form right after the call.  Added my Partners info (name, phone, address, etc) and case number.   Submitted.  Within minutes I get a VERY upset call from the office.  I’m asked why I “misrepresented” that this was MY divorce and why my Partner didn’t setup this consultation herself.

I was trying to help my Partner.  And I figured discussing her case with the attorney is what happens in the consultation, NOT on the phone with an office staffer.   I calmly explained that there was nothing fishy going on here, and handed the phone to my Partner, who was sitting nearby.   The office person continued to complain about our “sketchy” behavior, and told my Partner that “if I keep this consultation under NO circumstances can THAT MAN be on the phone, nor can you discuss your case with him”.  My Partner said “What are you talking about - he’s with me through this process”.

Bam - office staff cancelled consultation, refunded my payment, and let me know that they had cancelled everything due to “misrepresentation”.   Mind you - we literally spoke on the phone for 60 secs total.

Very strange - just be aware that this is the kind of bizarre, accusatory treatment you will likely receive if you choose to work with this firm.  Feels like I dodged a bullet, especially in light of the other reviews.

And yes, I did call the staff member disturbed, as her behavior was unhinged both to me and my Partner separately.   Never realized setting up a call appt for a Partner was going to set your staff off so badly.   Very unprofessional and others should understand what they are going to have to deal with if they reach out to you.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Kip.  We can't schedule with 3rd parties and you claimed that this was your divorce in our initial call.  It doesn't matter if you are speaking with a staff member or an attorney - we still need accurate information.  At no time in our call did we say "that man" or forbid your partner from speaking to you about the case, we told her that the attorney would need to speak to her directly and privately in order to ensure attorney client privilege.  You called our staff member "mentally ill" for trying to verify basic facts about this case and ensure that attorney client privilege remained intact for a consultation.

reviews.text: My experience was absolutely horrible the secretary is one of the rudest people I have ever met I had a person with me who needed help and wanted to make an appointment and he had walked to his office his wife just passed and this person had no compassion at allllll we called for an appointment like she asked and she cursed at me and hung up the phone I do not recommend this law firm to anyone.  They have no compassion or empathy and are heartess and rude. The gentleman I was helping said he had never been treated that way in his life.  Maybe this office should revaluate the way they treat people I would give 0 stars if I could.

You then went next door to an unrelated business and lied to them, telling them that you were a client of ours, knowing that you and your friend are not clients. We do not even practice the type of law for the case that you have. We do not allow anyone to scream at our office staff or behave threateningly to try to gain access inside of our office. reviews.text:
I cannot recommend Pro Legal Care highly enough. I was dealing with an exceptionally complex case involving a high-profile malignant narcissist who weaponized the legal system to inflict prolonged and costly abuse. The tactics were relentless—forum shopping, judge shopping, endless filings, perjury, and false police reports—all designed to drag litigation out indefinitely and make it as painful and invasive as possible.

I knew nothing about the Rockford courts—I don’t even live in Illinois. When it seemed like there was no end in sight to litigation, I switched attorneys and retained Pro Legal Care. From the start, their entire team was efficient, professional, and incredibly supportive.

Zach took on my case with a level of preparedness, legal knowledge, and strategic precision that immediately inspired confidence. He worked tirelessly, navigating every legal obstacle with skill and foresight, and ultimately secured the outcome we wanted. I truly cannot thank him enough.

I half jokingly mentioned to Zach that I planned to leave him an excellent review, but I had a bit of hesitation. I’d feel terrible if my review attracted others dealing with similarly nightmarish litigation. Zach’s response was telling: He welcomes challenging and complex cases because he knows the people in them need real help. That kind of dedication is rare.

If you need a lawyer who is strategic, relentless, and unshakably committed to securing the best possible outcome, Zach and Pro Legal Care are the ones to call.
reviews.ownerResponse: Valery, thank you so much. We're so happy we could help you find a new path forward.

reviews.text:
When I first hired Pro-Legal care, I was ultimately excited. They spoke as if they knew what they talking about, however I quickly learned if you have a high conflict case, this legal team is not as good to defend you. My ex was able to verbally abuse me through emails, and get away with a lot without anything being brought to the courts attention. Attorney Townsend also stated I could represent myself better than he could. In my opinion counsel was inadequate to appropriately speak in court when it actually mattered.  We both came to the conclusion for him to withdraw, and once he did I owed less than 2k, which I was actively making payments on. This law office was so quick to file motions and take me to court over money, even though I was paying but failed to properly get anything done in family court. I paid over 20K to this law office for them to provide me absolutely  nothing, and for them to also mess up my child support due to them resending motions to my job which caused confusion.

If you have a high-conflict case, I would HIGHLY suggest not using this law firm. For minor things that doesn't involve a high-conflict party and where the lawyer doesn't need to go head to head with the other party, and or Judge, hire them.

Edit to respond-
Actually, No I fully planned on leaving a review once I was completely done with your office, which was today. Please try not to scam anyone else of 20K without doing a proper jobs. You filed motions and nothing ever truly came from the motions due to not having a backbone. So for anyone in a HIGH CONFLICT case, should not choose this office. For minor things, I think you would be great. However, you allow the other party to verbally abuse your "client" amongst other stuff without bringing to the courts attention.  Best wishes to you guys as well, and try not to scam anyone else, at least with 20K someone else pays, at least get something completed for them.

reviews.text: If I could give a 0 star rating, I would. Townsend took advantage of a severely battered domestic violence victim’s vulnerability and ran with it. Interestingly, he didn’t even represent me in the family case. He only represented me in an order of protection case which resulted a temporary modification of an emergency order of protection in regards to my child. He lied to me about his intent to zealously advocate for me as a single mother whose mouth was wired shut from my jaw broken by my abuser. He continued to allow my abuser to have multiple continuances until I ran out of funds. It appears to be that he and my abuser’s attorney are well aquatinted considering the outcome of each and every court hearing. He even made it a point to humiliate me during my last hearing as his client in front of my abuser and his counsel. Prior to firing Townsend, Unusually, after hiring Townsend, he insisted to assert his appearance into the felony aggregated domestic violence criminal case on the account of my daughter as a protected party, however, that was not my initial intent of seeking his representation hired to do so. Therefore, he took it upon himself to take advantage of my vulnerable state and left a lot of unfulfilled promises of great representation during my paid phone call conversation (over $200 just to speak with him over the phone for an hour). He won’t even meet with you in person for a consultation and/or after retaining his services besides after your court hearing IF you even are required to show up in person instead of Zoom. Also, every request that I hired him for in the family matter, he did nothing for me. I had to represent myself pro se after firing him to even get child support ordered (that I had requested for months [and he would not take action] and retroactive back pay which I have documented proof of via email. Please seek other counsel because Zachary Townsend is a crook and is all about the money and could care less about the well being of his clients or the best interests of children involved. During the outrageous price to even get a consultation, he will sucker you in and make you think he will fight tooth and nail for you and then relentlessly drain your retainer fee and continue to allow continuances to rack up fees and before you know it your retainer is gone and you fall victim to the sunken cost fallacy if you decide to keep him as your lawyer. He will then sue you after giving over $6,000 to him after you give him notice that your funds are close to running out and will come up any excuse to keep billing you as the “victim” for services that I could have saved a lot of mine and my empathetic relative’s money. He is the poster child of the blood sucking attorney who will turn on you at the drop of a dime. 0 out of 10. Do not recommend! Please seek counsel elsewhere. If there was the possibility of having a public defender in a allocation of parental responsibility case, you would be better if with that option. However, save your hard earned money and BUYER BEWARE!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: We did not receive communication from you about any dissatisfaction about the course of your case, and your case was closed over six months ago. We understand that you are voicing your complaints about our services now that we are holding you to the payment terms of your contract. I hired Aaron Buscemi to represent me in a serious criminal case in Ogle County, Illinois. Over the course of nearly a year, he did virtually nothing to defend me, ignored repeated requests, and ultimately withdrew from my case after draining my retainer without filing a single meaningful motion.

He refused to challenge a deeply flawed search warrant, despite knowing that it omitted the fact that I am a registered Illinois medical cannabis patient—something that directly undermined the basis for the raid on my home. He also ignored my repeated requests for discovery, which I only obtained later through unrelated channels. He refused to submit seized evidence for independent testing, which could have significantly impacted the charges.

Even worse, he wouldn’t challenge an additional charge based on my wife's legally prescribed medication, which was clearly not mine and should never have been included in the case.

In the end, Mr. Buscemi withdrew from the case without having done any of the work a competent attorney should do, leaving me unrepresented at a critical time.

I would never recommend this attorney to anyone facing criminal charges. His neglect and inaction have had lasting consequences on my case and my life.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Respectfully, read this person’s other reviews, and I think you easily get a pretty good idea what really happened here.

Mr. Anderson was out of custody after his arrest strictly as a result of Attorney Buscemi's excellent work at his detention hearing. Very few defendants are released in Ogle County after being charged with offenses this serious, but against any reasonable expectation, Attorney Buscemi made that happen. From that day forward, this client wanted to spend endless time either arguing with his own lawyer about things THEY AGREED on, or telling Attorney Buscemi how he knew more than the lawyer he hired, based on Mr. Anderson's extensive consultations with the law firm of Google and Google. Not surprisingly, that apparently left very little time for Mr. Anderson to actually listen to his lawyer. If he had, he would have heard Mr. Buscemi when Mr. Anderson was told, repeatedly and among many other things: We couldn't file the appropriate motions directed at the evidence until we actually received complete discovery (a process only completed a few weeks before we moved to withdraw); Mr. Anderson had been provided properly redacted copies of all written discovery materials by us as we received and processed them (redaction is required by Supreme Court Rule, and to say he had to get them elsewhere later is an out and out lie); there was no reason to undertake the expensive (for the client) and complicated process of having the cannabis tested by an outside lab, because as a medical card holder it was perfectly legal for Mr. Anderson to possess the cannabis in the manner he did, regardless of the weight or specific variety; and we had already secured an agreement from the state to dismiss the prescription medication charge he says we "wouldn't challenge".

The staff in the Criminal Division at DeRango & Cain has been successfully defending clients in the most difficult criminal cases for over 60 years. Clients often ask for the impossible, and we do our best to make it happen anyway. But when your own client wants to question your judgment and to fight you every step of the way, even when their case is going very well, you have no choice but to recognize that you will never be able to make that client's demands and expectations align with reality. When that happens, the ethically responsible thing to do is part ways. Not a single thing about the stage of the proceedings could be described as a "critical time" when this situation left us no choice but to withdraw, dramatic flair notwithstanding.

Only a fool continues to represent a client who insists you're doing everything wrong, when you know without a doubt that you're doing everything exactly right. Mr. Anderson's conduct left us no choice, and his version of events is simply not accurate.

My husband and I began our immigration journey with Hoffmann Immigration Law back in 2020. Like many families starting this process, we had no idea what to expect—our only hope was to help my husband obtain his green card. From the moment we walked into their office, we felt welcomed, supported, and reassured.

Our first appointment was with Bethany, who took the time to explain every option, every step of the process, the pricing, and the expected timeline. She is incredibly knowledgeable, honest, and straightforward—qualities that we valued so much during such an emotional and uncertain time. She never sugar-coated anything; she always told us exactly what to expect, and because of that, we always felt prepared.

Throughout the entire process, the team was respectful, responsible, responsive, and truly by our side for every milestone. They helped us submit my husband’s visa and pardon applications, and though the pardon took about 3–4 years to be approved, the moment we received the approval letter everything moved quickly. Just one week later, we received the email with his appointment date at the American Consulate in Juarez. We couldn’t believe how fast everything was happening.

My husband was extremely nervous, but Johnathon, Bethany’s assistant, went above and beyond to prepare him for the interview. He practiced different types of questions with him, coached him on how to respond clearly, and helped him feel calm and confident. Stephanie also played a huge part—she prepared a complete packet with every document he needed for his interview in Juarez, organized and ready to go.

Because of their dedication, professionalism, and guidance, my husband completed the entire consulate process in about 15 days—and he was approved for his green card. We are still in disbelief. This is truly a dream come true for our family.

We are eternally grateful to Bethany, Stephanie, and Johnathon, and to everyone at Hoffmann Immigration Law. They changed our lives, and we will never forget what they did for us. If you are looking for immigration services, we highly and wholeheartedly recommend them.

Sincerely,
The Najera Family

Ok so this may be long but please read and by all means share: 5 years ago my father in law paid over $1000 to Attorney Laird Lambert to do a trust for him. At that time we were told when the time comes all we had to do was call him. When my father in law died I did just that. Mr. Lambert just said, well call me if you need anything, unfortunately I didn't know what I might need. After talking to our tax person I found out we needed a tax ID number for the trust so I called the attorney back. Mr. Lambert told me "my tax attorney could do that, or we could, or I guess I could" I responded that I would appreciate them taking care of it. Never heard back. So several weeks later I called and talked to secretary and she said "oh it's really easy you can just go online and do it" I advised her that Mr. Lambert stated when we set up this trust that he would take care of it...So fast forward a couple more messages left to today when I called again left another message. He called right back and I explained we need to get the tax id because we may be selling the house and need it. He said "call me when you have an offer" I said we already have one and I don't want waiting for the tax id to become an issue. He avoided question about tax id and went straight to telling me he could do the contract for the sale. I called back and said " that response was highly inappropriate and we weren't asking him to do anything for free we were asking him to follow up on what we paid him for 5 years ago. I have already contacted legal advice, left a message for the better business bureau, contacted the states attorney office, WREX TV, and the Attorney Registration and Disciplinary Board.

DO NOT USE THIS ATTORNEY!! He is rude and extremely unhelpful!!!

After the death of my Father I realized how important a will and trust were.  My will had been done years before and I didn't have a trust.  Laird was great to work with and very helpful. My wife and I met with him and expressed our wants and needs and he also offered other suggestions for us to consider.  Doing it without his help would have been very difficult.  He gave us step by step help, forms to get signed and have signed, how to get things done etc.

It was smooth, painless and affordable.  I have suggested his services to other members of my family.
Laird Lambert is a excellent Estate Planning Attorney. He is very sensitive to my every need.  I recommend him to anyone. Laird also does Elder Care and Real Estate. My initial consultation was free.

We recently moved cross-country from IL to AZ.

From the consultation to the final steps they were transparent and knowledgeable. No hidden fees, no surprises, and no excuses.
This legal team delivered the results I paid for.
I WILL be using them going forward as part of my IL legal team.
thank you again!

Mr. Gilbert has treated us so kindly. We've been to four other attorneys, and he's our fifth. He treats us with dignity and helps with advice too.

Mason Crosby was very sympathetic to the pain and heartache that we were suffering and listened to "our story" with open ears and an open heart. He told us of the many steps it would take, the Illinois Laws regarding grandparent visitation, and the pros and cons to a case. Mason was very professional in all of our court proceedings, on the phone and in meetings.

We worked very closely with his paralegal Brittany Matz who is nothing short of AMAZING! She worked diligently to try to help us achieve our goal of seeing our granddaughter and spent many long hours on our case, all the while keeping us "in the loop" of any information that arose.

In the end my husband and I did drop the case as the Illinois Law for grandparents is just tough, but Mason went out of his way to speak with interested parties, and we did get a visit from our granddaughter after a year and a half of nothing, which we will be forever grateful for.

Bottom line...hire this firm!!

reviews.text:
My divorce was finalized yesterday, and I'm taking the time to reflect today. Divorce is difficult. I originally didn't want to get a lawyer because of costs, but as someone who doesn't want to deal with legal terminology, I needed one.

Right from the beginning, working with Crosby & Crosby has been amazing. Lisa found the necessary information to get the process of divorce started. Niki was amazing throughout the entire thing. Guiding me and providing me with factual advice, and reminders to get through the parenting class, parenting plan, financial affidavit, marriage settlement agreement, and judgement. Mason provided great insight, and let me know all of the options that I had. I initially found all things with court daunting, but after every conversation I felt at ease and fully informed with every decision I made. They also gave me the freedom and support to make this difficult journey as smooth as possible, by supporting me with having the difficult conversation with my ex-husband. I wanted this process to be as amicable as possible, and with their help, that's exactly the result that I got.

Thank you to Lisa, Niki, and Mason for guiding me on this difficult and painful journey. I truly could not have gotten through this as quickly as I could have without all of your help. I hope that you know how much weight you all have taken off my shoulders by representing me.

Crosby law firm is one of the worst law firms in Illinois. We have been on a probate case for four years with no ending in sight. They had all the financials for the estate last year in May and they were supposed to turn them into the judge on June 1, 2022. It never happened now we have a different judge and this is still going on and I don’t understand. Now Crosby withdrawal from the case, so now we have to get a new lawyer. I’m sure whom ever we get they will be better then Crosby. I could’ve represented us better and it would’ve been done years ago. These lawyers at Crosby  do nothing for you. reviews.ownerResponse:
Unfortunately this conversation is necessary. Originally, I had no idea who this person was or what "neighbor" she allegedly was associated with. Jeannie was never my client, nor had I ever met, spoken, or otherwise communicated with her.

Coincidentally, the very next morning following Jeannie's post I had an appointment with a client and his adult son (from out of state) to pick up copies of documents from prior years and go over what had been accomplished at that time. My client believed that someone had broken into his house and stolen many of the documents I had prepared, and he needed replacements. About 5 minutes into that meeting, a woman barged into my office, interrupted the meeting, and demanded my client and his son leave due to another appointment. I asked for her name, and she responded "Jeannie". I asked if she was the individual who had left the one-star Google review, and she smiled and said yes. We asked her to leave so we could finish our meeting and she did. My client, his son, and I completed the meeting in another 15 minutes.

My work for the client corresponded to rough times for him and his wife, spanning a few years and involving estate planning, asset transfers, and the preparation of probate-related documents following his wife's passing nearly two years ago.

Contrary to Jeannie's comment, the Will clearly deals with my client's assets and heirs. And pertaining to Jeannie's comment that the "county clerk said this is a mess", employees in the county clerk offices do not give legal advice and there are signs clearly posted saying they do not give such advice.

My client told me he never authorized Jeannie to leave a Google review, that she never should have written the review, and he was going to ask her to remove it. My client and his son thanked me for all of my work at the conclusion of the meeting, we all shook hands, and they left.

About an hour later my client came back into the office and asked to use the phone. He said that he and his son were driven to my office by Jeannie, and she had stranded them there. This is a harsh reminder to exercise care in trusting and involving others in one's important life events.

I thank my client and his family for entrusting me with advising him in the face of major life events, and his wife, he, and I worked together during difficult times.

reviews.ownerResponse: This new client made an appointment to discuss the death of her ex-husband (divorce), the ex-husband's not having a will, and the family's alleged dissatisfaction with how her former step-daughter was handling the probate estate as executor.  I confirmed in the court records that two weeks prior to our meeting, family members signed documents consenting to the appointment of the former step-daughter as executor, these waivers were filed with the court, and the judge relied on these documents to appoint my client's former step-daughter as executor.  I explained the "intestate" (no will) probate process in detail, including who would receive assets based on intestacy, and who could potentially serve as executor.    I also explained that since the former step-daughter had only been appointed two weeks earlier (with the family's written consent), it was highly unlikely that the judge would remove her from the executorship unless there was evidence of significant self-dealing or incompetence.  I told the ex-wife that there were two alternatives:  1) officially challenge the appointment of the executor by filing a motion in court, which would be financially costly with no guarantee of success, and 2) I could reach out to the executor's attorney, discuss the bases for the family's dissatisfaction, and see if the executor would commit to more open communication.  The ex-wife was not interested in incurring the cost of an official challenge to her former step-daughter's executorship, and asked me to take the more personal route of speaking with the executor's attorney to communicate openly with family members about how the executor was dealing with estate issues.  I offered my client a proposal for limiting payment for my work to only one hour of time, which included the nearly 1.5 hours we had met for at my office and the future work I would accomplish, and she agreed and paid at the end of the meeting.  I spoke with the executor's attorney the next day, and that attorney informed me that she would speak with her client about this the following week during a pre-scheduled meeting.  I relayed that information to my client.  Unfortunately, in the meantime my client communicated with the executor's attorney directly, and made accusations against the executor.  Needless to say, the executor's attorney was no longer helpful in attempting to calm the waters, and my client did not ask me to officially challenge the executor's appointment.  Instead, my client posted her negative views about my handling of the situation on Google.  I hope the family's turmoil is just a misunderstanding and the probate case proceeds smoothly.

reviews.text: Jim Meason is by far the worst attorney I've ever encountered in over 20 years in the real estate business. On a $67,000 property that was a cash purchase he charged my $1000 because I was an out of state buyer which was a high fee on average for attorneys in that area, but I obliged. I put down $1000 as an earnest money deposit through Mr. Meason and paid him his $1000 fee as well. The home inspection revealed that the home was in no condition to be sold, so I instructed Jim to void the contract and return me my earnest money deposit. For that alone he charges me $1525.00 and returns me only $475.00 of my earnest money deposit. He charged over 1500 dollars for a house that he didn't even have to close on after he initially stated that his fee is usually 500 dollars but this time would be 1000 because I was an out of state buyer. Mind you this was all within a 2 week period. Its not like he had to do much more than send a few emails and make a few phone calls and get my earnest money returned to me. He is horrible at getting back to his clients in a timely manner, sometimes taking more than 3 business days to respond and never picks up his phone. He is a con man....do yourself the favor and stay away from him unless you have money to be scammed out of.
reviews.ownerResponse: Sometimes, clients' transactions do not go the way they had planned.  Just after Christmas, Mr. Lopez, a New Jersey client, hired me to protect his interests in a pending real estate purchase.  He had signed a contract on an Illinois single family home, site unseen and without the benefit of an attorney, for the purpose of owning the property as an investment.  Just a day or two before he was about to lose the contract to another bidder because he had not paid the contractually-required $1,000 earnest money deposit (and the day before he was leaving for a New Year's Eve vacation in the Bahamas), he called me to assist him on the recommendation of a local realtor.  Due to the impending loss of the property and his imminent vacation, I immediately reviewed the contract and provided Mr. Lopez with a summary of his rights and obligations under Illinois law, and suggested I prepare an amendment to the contract (extending the dates for him to schedule a home inspection and to void the contract, if necessary due to an unsatisfactory inspection).  I then prepared the contract amendment, wrote a check on my account to cover the earnest money deposit, drove it to the listing agent's office, and paid it on Mr. Lopez' behalf.  Later, when the property inspection report revealed the home's poor condition, I advised Mr. Lopez on how I could void the contract and avoid forfeiting the earnest money (under the terms of the contract amendment I had implemented).  After dealing with the listing agent's questions about salvaging the deal through a price reduction or repairs, and communicating with Mr. Lopez about these options, I confirmed Mr. Lopez was not interested in the property.  I then secured a full earnest money deposit refund and picked up the check at the listing agent's office.  My time and efforts were considerable, and I successfully represented Mr. Lopez in a professional manner.  I thank him for that opportunity.

reviews.text:
As a new business owner who has been preparing for the launch of my shop for over six months, I expected my legal representation to understand and support my need to focus primarily on my enterprise, especially for non-critical matters like traffic violations. Unfortunately, this was not the case. Despite clearly communicating that I should not need to appear in court for minor traffic issues—since that was the purpose of hiring legal aid—my preferences were overlooked, and the advice given was simply to align with the court’s expectations.

Further compounding the issue, essential documents I was supposed to receive remain undelivered, and my lawyer was absent for a scheduled court date without any prior explanation. As of the date of this review,
( 5/21/24 ) I have paid for services that were not fully rendered, and whether I will receive a refund remains uncertain.

This experience has been disheartening, especially during a critical phase of establishing my business. I had hoped for professional guidance that would allow me to manage my legal concerns without detracting from my business, but the service fell short of those expectations.

I am now seeking other legal services and advise potential clients to carefully consider these experiences when choosing this firm for legal representation
Very professional and to the point!!

I don’t know which lawyer I spoke to from this law firm but they were totally and utterly unprofessional. I spoke on the phone regarding a case with a family member. They were put in Winnebago County Jail, in a suicide cell and the guards were withholding water, the cell didn’t have a working toilet, they withhold a mattress and didn’t let the inmate out of the cell for an hour, to shower, or at all, during their 3 days there. The innate allegedly broke a sprinkler in the cell but given the cruel and unusual punishment, I asked the lawyer if, I was to hire him, if he could subpoena the surveillance footage from the suicide cell. The lawyer proceeded to argue with me for several minutes about how they “dont keep cameras inside jail cells” I calmly tried to explain to him that they do in fact keep cameras inside the suicide cells, unlike the normal part of a jail. You would think a criminal defense lawyer and a lawyer that handles lawsuits would have enough sense to know that. I guess that inflated his big ego cause he proceeded to get mad at me and told me that even if they had surveillance footage, that it would be irrelevant to the case which is honestly laughable. Personally, if a lawyer can be that incompetent, unprofessional, and emotional in a short 5 minute phone call, then I certainly would not trust him with a life altering legal case. Regardless of opinions, there is never a reason to get mouthy and disrespectful with someone for no reason like that. Hopefully this will help the next person.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Kimberly, we are sorry to hear that you feel dissatisfied with your experience. Our firm takes client service and communication very seriously, and feedback—both positive and critical—is important to us.

Legal matters can sometimes involve multiple attorneys and procedural steps depending on the circumstances of the case, scheduling, and the specific legal issues involved. This is done to ensure that clients receive the appropriate legal attention and resources needed for their matter.

Because attorney-client matters are confidential, we cannot discuss case details in a public forum. However, we are committed to addressing any concerns directly and professionally. She was kind, patient, and incredibly understanding. Even without being a current client, she took the time to listen, answer my questions, and make sure I felt supported and informed. Her professionalism combined with genuine compassion immediately put me at ease.

As a paralegal who works closely with multiple law firms, I can confidently say that Crow Law stands out in the best possible way. Watching their team operate is genuinely impressive—they are organized, proactive, and consistently meet every deadline without exception.

Crow Law handles every task with precision and professionalism. I would not hesitate to recommend Crow Law to anyone seeking a firm that truly delivers exceptional work.

Always when you want it done right.

Finally being heard and taken seriously by the courts! Finally winning and getting relief!
Little back story. I have had multiple family court cases over 7 years with little to no relief on my own. Desperate, this firm was recommended by a family member who had crow law against her (they won that case too); I made the decision to get an attorney and hired Attorney Crow and Attorney Holder.
I had a very complex custody issues over the years.  Crow law took on Order of protections, guardianship, adoption, seek a parental of rights termination, child support, termination of guardianship, and now contempt of court. They took the time to understand how all it was connected and come up with a quick plan. They were never confused though there were so many cases. I couldn't do that on my own even though I tried many times.
Working with Crow Law was easy from the start; they did all the work and guided me on the way. They made me feel supported from day one. Even going as far as to look over the dockets and evidence of the cases to make sure that they were the right people for the case and this was something they could do. They were honest about my shortcomings and always portrayed me in a positive light. They were honest that we had a long road ahead. They made sense of it all. They never once gave me false hope. They never devalued me for my previous decisions and always guided me through the process.
I was absolutely clueless, not taken seriously by the courts, and very confused. Now that I have crow law i feel seen heard by courts and fully supported by crow law. I now understand the cases.
Upon hired I quickly got assigned to Niccole and Shelby who have always been fantastic and timely with my questions. If they don't understand what I send or say they always make sure to get clarification with me to make sure that they understand what I am saying. They truly understand law and all that comes with that. They also treat everyone they work with as family and with grace.
They are cautious when it comes to legal fees as well as well we know that the pricing over time always add up. I feel that the fees definitely speak for the quality of their work. I recommend them to anyone who needs an family attorney time in and time out. Thank you crow law for all u do.

Adrian was extremely nice. She talked with all of those involved in my case and even got my son to small talk with her. In the end being appointed her law firm, made my son an overly happy young man.❤️

The case went for a few months, there were a few times I had to press to get a response or the correct paperwork. Repeating some things and correcting simple errors.

HOWEVER, we were so ready to have the case ended and over with. My husband had to leave work each time and then return, I had to bring my son into school late a few times and find a sitter for my youngest each time. So, yeah. We were done by the end. And we understood we were not the only case being cared for at the time.

Pricing was around where my husband had assumed it would be as well.

All in all, our experience wasn’t bad. I do recommend if doing adoption for spouse and child, help stay on top of your case.
After 30 days of finalization, it becomes more steps to have all proper paperwork you need to change things and more waiting on papers to be signed from judge to be able to receive all your documents. Communication and responses move it along faster. 😁

In total, Adrian did appear to be totally invested and passionate about her position involving the happiness and well being of my son in this situation.

So, thank you for all of your and your staffs help in making my son one happy dude and our family an official crew.
🥰💜💙💛🩵💖💚🥰

Niccole has been extremely awesome to work with and very professional. The results all her efforts working on my case has been very positive. reviews.text:
Our experience with this firm was extremely disappointing. The secretaries were completely inadequate and lacked any sense of communication or coordination. I reached out to three different secretaries on multiple occasions for simple, non-legal questions—things that clearly fell within their job duties—and each time I was told they either “didn’t know” or that it “wasn’t their job.”

The only person in that office who demonstrated any professionalism or integrity was Veronica. She’s the only one who seemed to understand her responsibilities and genuinely tried to help.

When we finally decided to terminate our case due to the lack of support, the office continued trying to schedule unnecessary appointments instead of addressing our refund request. I asked for copies of any work completed on our case, but all they could provide were the same documents we had originally submitted to them.

The attorney, Omar, refused to take accountability. He blamed his assistant, paralegal, and even his fiancée (who handles finances) for the miscommunication, and ultimately denied our refund by hiding behind a so-called “non-refundable deposit” of $750. However, the retainer agreement clearly states that if either party terminates before completion of work, they reserve the right to charge only for work completed up to that point. No actual work was done on our case—our advance parole process was never started—so there was no justification for keeping the deposit.

Despite multiple emails to his staff formally requesting termination and a refund, each one told me that the final decision was up to Omar. He later claimed no one had ever informed him about our refund requests, which is simply not true.

Overall, this office demonstrated a complete lack of professionalism, accountability, and ethics. I would strongly caution anyone seeking immigration help to look elsewhere—especially if you value communication, honesty, and transparency.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Karen,
We’re sorry to hear that your experience with our office did not meet your expectations. Our goal is always to provide clear communication and professional service to every client.

We understand that your concerns have already been reviewed and a resolution was reached. Unfortunately, this issue was caused by factors outside of our control and already discussed. While we regret that you remain dissatisfied, we appreciate your feedback and will continue using it to improve our processes and client experience.

We wish you the best moving forward.

— The Law Offices of Omar Salguero

reviews.ownerResponse:
Mr. Brauberger, you wrote a completely fake and false review. Our firm has never represented anyone by the name of Duke Brauberger. Therefore, either you placed your review against the wrong law firm OR you intentionally wrote a fake review to harm our firm's reputation. Evidence that it is a fake review is below.

1) Unnatural Language: Words like "languishes" and "expeditiously" are not typically used in everyday speech or reviews.
2) Inconsistent Tone: Emotional outbursts mixed with awkward phrasing which is typical of fabricated reviews.
3) No Specifics: Authentic reviews typically include constructive criticism with personal examples.

We work extremely hard for all of our clients to get the best results possible. As proof, we ask everyone to read all of the legitimate reviews from our former clients, all of which are posted along with this fake review.

Mr. Brauberger, if you posted this review in error and it was meant for another law firm, we are hopeful that you will remove the review and post it where it belongs. If it was written intentionally to harm our reputation, then we will pursue all of our resources to find your true identity and prosecute our claims for libel and defamation against you.

reviews.text: Last summer, I lost my dad in a motorcycle accident. It was pretty clear from the beginning that it was the other driver’s fault. But, as you can imagine, I was overwhelmed. I knew I should hire a lawyer to take care of handling the other guy’s insurance company. I put off finding a lawyer for a month or so because I was in an information overload stage. I shouldn’t have waited.  David Brassfield handled my wrongful death case against the other driver’s insurance company and I could not have picked a better lawyer to represent me. Brassfield knew what he was doing and he also put everything in terms that I could understand, which is so important for someone going through something that’s already overwhelming. He took all the pressure off of me and updated me regularly about new reports or correspondence. I went into this thinking it could take years, and maybe with another lawyer it would have. But not with Brassfield. He worked diligently to work out a settlement and it took far less time than I thought it would to reach one that I am happy with. Brassfield made sure I understood every direction we could go with the case as well. He also cared that I lost my father and regularly made sure I was doing OK. That’s the kind of person you want to represent you. If you’re looking for someone to handle a wrongful death case, I highly recommend Brassfield and Krueger.
reviews.ownerResponse: Rachel, thank you for taking the time to write about your experience with Brassfield & Krueger, Ltd. during such a difficult time. Losing a loved one in a tragic accident can be incredibly overwhelming and we understand how important it is to have someone who can handle the legal aspects with both compassion and competence. Although no amount of money can compensate you for the loss of your father, I’m glad that my efforts helped you receive a fair settlement in a timely fashion. Thank you again for sharing your experience and please know that we are here for you if you ever need assistance in the future. – Dave Brassfield

Thank you Dave
reviews.ownerResponse:
Elaine, thank you very much for taking the time to leave such a detailed account of your experience with our office. It was a pleasure to represent you in this case. The reason I asked about you every time I called is because I truly care about your well being. I felt so sorry for the injuries you received. I’m glad I was able to make it easy for you to concentrate on recovering from those injuries. I know the liability situation looked bleak at the beginning, as you were making a left hand turn in front of an oncoming vehicle. I’m so glad I was able to turn the liability situation around and prove the other vehicle was at fault for running the red light. We would not have been able to do this if you had not retained our services soon after the accident. As both vehicles were totaled and about to be demolished, it was imperative that I had an opportunity to retain an expert to review the black boxes in both vehicles before they were demolished. Had you waited a few weeks to retain our services, the evidence in the vehicles(black boxes) would have been destroyed. We would not have been able to prove our case, as you had no memory of the accident and no witness was able to testify as to the color of the lights. I am so glad I was able to prove you were not at fault, and to help you get a very nice settlement for your severe injuries. I’m also thankful that you made a great recovery. Please call any time you need anything.
Dave

reviews.text: I was working for an online food delivery company when I fell on a woman's property.  Originally, I thought I could sue the property owner.  Fortunately, I had Atty Monteleone on my case.  Through his expert knowledge and experience with  the law, he determined that I actually could sue three separate parties.  In addition, my medical expenses were around $58,000, and Atty Monteleone negotiated that amount down to about $18,000.  Wow!!! He also researched and found a lessor known Illinois law that allowed me to sue the delivery company I was working for, and they paid about half of the $18,000.  Atty Monteleone always returned my phone calls, texts, and emails within an hour, even on weekends.  He did such a wonderful job and took care of everything, and I could just rest and heal at home.  No worries.  Atty Monteleone is very professional, knowledgeable, and sincerely cares about his clients.

David was great start to finish very professional and knowledgeable. He kept me updated through the whole process never once did I have any doubt he is hands down the best attorney . Could not thank him enough .

reviews.text:
We learned the hard way that we should have went with these guys first. We did not because of a recommendation that came to us. The first attorneys we went with (Macloskey and Kessler) gave us nothing but the runaround for what had to have been at least a couple months. Every time we would call them to get some sort of update, we would get some BS that completely avoided what we called for in the first place. That was another problem, they would never call us. This is when we had finally had enough. I remembered hearing about Fisk and Monteleone for years on local TV, so we gave them a call and were impressed from the very first call. We knew we found the right attorneys that actually wanted to help us. David, John, and their staff were very caring and always were keeping us updated on things. Sometimes they would even call to say there weren't any updates. They knew time was of the essence, especially after a couple months had already went by since our accident happened. Within the first week, John and David had already gotten more done than our last attorneys did in a couple months. The whole experience with this team was amazing from start to finish and I cant recommend them enough. My wife and I were actually a little sad when everything was settled. Because of how well we were treated and how much they cared, it almost felt like family we would probably never see again. To the Fisk and Monteleone team, my wife and I cant thank you all enough for everything you did for us.
Josh & Liz

reviews.text: These are the attorneys you want on your side with any injury claim!  Mr. Monteleone started our case with a very slim, if non existent, chance of getting a settlement. reviews.ownerResponse:
We sincerely apologize for your recent experience with our firm. We understand how challenging and disheartening it can be to navigate the complexities of disability claims, especially after facing denials.

Regarding our policy, we must clarify that our firm typically does not take cases where the claimants are currently working. This policy stems from our historical data showing a significantly lower success rate in cases where clients have ongoing employment. Although the government allows part-time work while receiving Social Security benefits, our track record in these scenarios has not been favorable.

We regret if this policy and its implications were not communicated to you empathetically and clearly. Our goal is always to provide the best possible guidance, even when we cannot take on a case.

If you have further questions or need more detailed explanations, please don’t hesitate to contact Shawn, who will be more than willing to assist you. Again, we are sorry for any frustration or inconvenience caused and appreciate your understanding of our position.

Sincerely,
Shawn Good
The Good Law Group
