reviews.text:
There are two reasons to hire the Good Law Group to represent you in your SSDI case:
1. They literally wrote the book on the subject.
They co-authored a book that is a literal blueprint to navigating the SSDI system. It includes most of the questions that the ALJ (administrative law judge) will ask.
When you go to a doctor, or need an operation, extensive physical therapy or treatment, you want a healthcare provider with experience. To use a baseball analogy, you want the guys with the most plate appearances. You want the guys that have seen all the different pitches from all the different pitchers. You want the guys that have been in all the different ball parks, and know which way the wind blows. Most importantly, you want the guys that know all the different umpires, and what their strike zones are. That’s what comes with experience, and The Good guys know all the umpires.

2. Sean and Neil have set the standard for SSDI law practices  in the NW suburbs. However, they couldn’t offer and maintain those standards without an outstanding administrative office staff. It takes an experienced team to navigate all of the paperwork and medical records that are the foundation of each case.
The tip of the spear in that effort is their administrative office manager Ashley. With all due respect to Sean and Neil, but Ashley is the rock star at the practice, and I’d say that without her, it would be impossible to handle all the cases with the professionalism and attention to detail that they provide. I’m quite certain she deserves a raise. 🙂
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to leave us this amazing review! We appreciate you trusting us to handle your case. Please do not hesitate to reach out if you need anything going forward.

-Shawn Good

After years of dealing with a difficult family court situation, Brad and his team helped bring clarity and resolution. Brad was firm, fair, and extremely knowledgeable throughout the entire process. He understood the details of my case, stayed focused on strategy, and handled court proceedings with confidence. He didn’t overpromise — he executed.

Ryan, his paralegal, was exceptional. Organized, professional, and proactive. He kept everything moving, made sure deadlines were met, and responded quickly whenever I had questions. In family court, where details matter and emotions can run high, having someone steady and on top of the paperwork makes a real difference.

If you’re looking for a family law team that combines legal strength with professionalism and clear communication, I highly recommend Brad and Ryan. I’m grateful for their guidance and the outcome we achieved.

Bradley Tengler was honest from the start and let me know my case could be challenging, but he still chose to take it on and fight for me. That meant a lot during such a difficult time.

His communication was always clear, thorough, and consistent. He explained every step of the process and made sure I understood my options. My case involved a DCFS matter following a domestic dispute, and it was very emotional and overwhelming. Bradley handled everything with professionalism, compassion, and strong advocacy.

Because of his dedication and hard work, the case was resolved in my favor. I am extremely grateful and highly recommend Bradley Tengler to anyone needing an attorney who is responsive, honest, and truly committed to their clients.

My experience with the Bradley Tengler Law firm was fantastic! Bradley and his Paralegal Ryan handled our guardianship case with the professionalism that went beyond our expectations, They advised us of the procedures that we should anticipate and the possible outcomes. We attended a court hearing, they represented us and the judge, based on what was presented granted us guardianship of our two great grandson’s. Thank you Bradley and Ryan, you are the best!

Mr. Bradley Tengler and Mr. Ryan Smith kept me informed with any updates of my case and stress free. Brad helped me with legal advice , kept it professional and diligent with my case . I strongly recommend their team to any individual looking for top notch legal representation for child custody or any legal matters ! I am grateful for his representation . Thank you .

I can’t thank Attorney Tengler enough for his support during a difficult and uneasy time. He made the entire family law process feel seamless, even when things were a tad overwhelming. It was clear at every step that he knew exactly what he was doing. His assistant, Ryan, was also fantastic — responsive, helpful, and great to work with!

If you're looking for a family law attorney who truly cares and will guide you with both professionalism and compassion, I highly recommend Attorney Tengler!

I hired Bradley in 2021 for a guardianship case to regain custody of my son. Despite the challenges within the legal system, he helped me successfully regain shared custody. Not long after achieving shared custody, juvenile court became involved, and I was ultimately awarded sole custody.

Regardless of the mixed reviews you might come across, I can confidently say that Bradley and Ryan are an excellent legal team. Their patience and understanding throughout the court process are exactly what you need during such a difficult time. I’ll be working with them again for an upcoming relocation case. They genuinely advocate for their clients and do their best to represent parents with integrity and commitment, especially when a child’s well-being is at stake.

My experience was hell, I was left out to dry. I was told I’ll have a fair shot at reducing my child support. But when the time came. He basically told me to kick rocks. I also was told by my child’s mother. That my attorney, Bradley. Was making not so great gestures about me. In all I thank him for his services. But nothing he did helped me get to where I’m at now!
We had an amazing experience with Brad and Ryan through our adoption case. Brad truly shows that he always has the best interest of the child as a priority. No complaints, HIGHLY recommend!!

I've been working with Brad since my wife filed divorce 1-1/2 yrs ago. I can't say enough about his approach, honesty, demeanor, legal knowledge, staff and how he has made a bad experience into the best it could be. I'm not even done yet with my situation yet but look no further.

By far the worst lawyer I've ever Delt with he sent a criminal lawyer lady to my court date who was always late. As in always I mean the 2/3 time she even showed up. Then  Mr. tengler shows up to extreamly disrespectful  to me my mom and my ex girlfriend. I explained my problems I was having with him and his staff he removed himself from my case after he was paid 3k then he has the nerve to call my mother and demand another 500$ .

I have been researching lawyers in the Winnebago area for a while now. Brad's profile made it seem as though he was a person who could do mediation to help parties agree. I have had much success with my ex in coming to mutual agreements, but I just needed extra assistance for another matter. By the time of our consultation, Mr. Tengler had already made up his mind and would wait to listen to my case details. He went into custody issues (which isn't even what this consultation was about), which proved that he needed to take the time to look at my cases. I provided case numbers, and Mr. Tengler could have easily seen that these were all post-divorce enforcement. He claimed my case was too contentious and that there was no way I would be getting more for child support even though my ex's finances went up by way more than 20%. All previous issues with the cases had ended with agreements, but he stated that my cases were too contentious. It was so disappointing because I was looking forward to working with him to solve these issues outside of the courtroom; even listening to his podcasts, I agreed with his morals and ideas. I ended the consultation early because he had already decided and didn't care about what I was looking to do. This matter MOST DEFINITELY could have been handled outside of the courts. You told me that I wouldn’t get anything more for child support even though his income went up by more than 20%. That is a percentage dictated by Illinois law. You lower arrears. But you didn’t want to hear any of that.

Single mother warning- He will not fight for the rights of you or your children.
reviews.ownerResponse: Megan, I see that you were disappointed by what I had said during your initial phone consultation with me. I understand that this kind of litigation can be exhausting and wears on committed parents, such as yourself. My intent during an initial consultation is to be frank - about the cost of litigation, reasonable outcomes, and how our law office functions. This allows both myself and potential clients to know upfront whether we are the right fit and creates a smoother attorney-client relationship during the intensity that can arise if settlement fails and litigation is necessary. I wish you the best as you move forward and success in the courtroom.

No one gets married to get a divorce, but it happens! Getting a divorce can be an extremely overwhelming roller-coaster ride but if you have children involved it can be an emotional, financial, and mental nightmare that you wouldn’t wish on your worst enemy. Furthermore, you must have an extraordinary and proficiently skilled attorney that can ease the burden of your battle, storm, and earthly hell. Brad Tengler knows the law and applies his amazing expert scope of practice in the court of law to fight for the best outcome in your divorce and/or child custody battle. Brad Tengler (and his amazing team (Ryan)) assured me that the storm would pass, a resolution would be reached, and life would resume to a new normal (and it did). Overall, if you need an attorney that will fight and represent you in court to ease and win the battle in your horrendous storm of a divorce and/or child custody, I highly recommend Brad Tengler!!!

Brad & Ryan are a awesome team !
My divorce was a lil complex at first but Brad assured me along the way he was supportive !
If you want someone who cares about their client he is it…Ryan is the best as well !

I’m a civil litigation attorney and went to law school with Brad.  I had tried to handle my own divorce case to save money, but didn’t realize how time-consuming it was and missed a deadline resulting in dismissal of my case. I finally reached out to Brad and he immediately picked up all the pieces, figured things out, got my case back on file, worked collaboratively with my ex, and got us a final, amicable resolution all in just a few months. I’m extremely grateful for Brad’s help on this and would highly recommend him for anyone in need of divorce or family law-related legal services.

Would never recommend him. Save yourself the frustration and find someone else.

He failed to conduct a conflict of interest check at the beginning of my case. Turns out he had previously represented a party about 10 years ago and wasn't even aware of it until a few weeks into the case.

Several times he asked me for names and contact information for people when I had given him this information on the very first day I hired him as my attorney, showing that he either didn't take notes or just didn't care to look them up in my file.

He didn't return the remainder of my retainer until I asked his office for it when I learned that I was entitled to the remainder (thank God for law school).

Finally, he never. responded. to my last email asking him if I could retrieve my daughter after my case was over. It's been 9 months and I still haven't heard from him. I had to rely on the information my husband received from his attorney to know that I could get my daughter back.

It seemed like he relied on my husband's attorney to disseminate information to me several times throughout the case. As my attorney he should have been the one updating me.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ashley, I can see that your experience was very frustrating. I strive to provide the best representation possible for every case. But I am always open to feedback, particularly in light of the concerns you raised. I would be glad to speak with you further, if you would like to do so. Please let me know and we will get a time set up.
Brad

reviews.text: I was facing some serious charges and Jason Tempin got my charges down from a class 3 felony to a misdemeanor. He was persistent and driven. He even scheduled routine office meetings to update me on the status of the case and what he expected of me. Highly recommend Bulldog legal services as your go to attorney office to handle your case.

I was involved in an ostensible situation where I was portrayed as the antagonist. The video evidence was against me, multiple eye witnesses testified against me, and I was charged with 2 felony counts. I was facing serious charges, potential years in prison, probation, and incredible fines.
Attorney Jason Tempin handled my case. I chose Jason because he was the only attorney who actually called me back to ask if I wanted his help, and this was after we already had an hour long phone consultation. No other attorney did that. He called me and wanted me to not feel pressured but to give assurance that he was ready and willing to take on my case when i was ready.
He was not only highly skilled and knowledgeable regarding legal proceedings, but was empathetic and reasonable while understanding the complexities of the case. When i was engaged with him he never rushed or made me feel that his time was more valuable. He was there for you and listened intently. He is a man of integrity and told me, "Ethically, I do not want to put you in a place or situation that makes you feel uncomfortable regarding any agreement or outcome" from the prosecution.
Like I said, I was facing serious charges and Jason with his expert negotiating skills managed to Dismiss my case with prejudice. It was a stunning outcome and even the judge told me, as he smiled from the bench, "This doesn't happen very often".
The staff at Bulldog Legal services were friendly and expeditious. They were available and communicated all information with urgency. If you're in the office please admire the paintings too.
I can not say enough about how happy I am that I chose Jason Tempin to represent me and handle my case. He was there in the court for every court hearing that i had and made sure to shepard me through the whole process with ease of mind. I am forever grateful to Jason for keeping my life intact and I will be returning for his assistance in expunging this from my record.
If, god forbid, I am ever in trouble, I know I can rely on Jason to help mitigate any legal issues. I truly can not recommend him enough. 5 stars
Exceeded expectations and very comforting experience with good dedicated people 20 out of 10!

If you want to pay someone $3,500 just to be screamed at and thrown out of their office, then this is the lawyer for you. I hired him to handle my case, but during what should have been a routine office visit, he lost his temper simply because I was wearing a beer T-shirt under a zipped hoodie. When I calmly told him I didn’t appreciate being spoken to that way—especially after paying him thousands of dollars—he threw my folder across the desk, told me it was “16 hours of work” and that I wouldn’t be getting anything back, then ordered his secretary to kick me out.

I never received a refund, never got proper legal help from him, and ultimately had to hire another attorney (Albert Altamore) to fix the mess. This incident happened about a year ago, and I waited until all my court matters were resolved before posting this review to make sure he couldn’t jeopardize my case.

I’m writing this now to warn others: save your money, time, and peace of mind.

reviews.ownerResponse: Hi, Nasr: Our consultation fee policy is that if a person schedules a follow up appointment within 6 months of their initial consult, we do not charge another consultation fee. We also limit the kinds of cases we take so each case receives the attention it needs. According to our records, you had an initial consultation in April 2022, scheduled a follow up in July 2022 but did not show up for your appointment. You are now calling us three years after your initial consultation. Over the last three years we have hit our capacity for asylum cases. We wish you would have kept your appointment in July 2022 when we had capacity to assist you in your legal matter at that time.

My mother's green card lapsed while she was abroad, due to returning to her home country to take care of her aging mother. In 2016, my family wanted to start the process of bringing my mom back to the US; even before the election, it was so difficult to find an immigration attorney who was available and who I felt comfortable with. My husband met Sara Dady through volunteering, and I/my mother became a client shortly thereafter. For the next few years--through changing immigration rules and a global pandemic--Sara and her team (shout out to Lora!) were a steady, reliable presence and always prompt and thorough in responses. They answered any questions I or my parents had, and helped us focus on the important parts of my mom's case and feel confident in the process. My mom was granted permission to return to the US in June and came back/received her green card in August. I could not be more grateful to Sara and to Dady Law Group.
